summary: Fiery Okie cover, tight Hard To Handle jam, high‑energy set with Beach Boys guests
blurb: The show delivers a scorching "Okie From Muskogee*" and a fierce "Hard To Handle" that keep the momentum rolling. Jerry’s guitar is on fire throughout, while Pigpen’s organ adds grit to "Searchin'*" and the closing "Turn On Your Lovelight" shines.
review: April 27 1971 at the Fillmore East remains one of those oddball nights where the Dead’s rock‑and‑roll drive collides with a surprise Beach Boys cameo. The first set kicks off strong with "Truckin'", "Mama Tried" and a ripping "Hard To Handle" that showcases Jerry Garcia’s ferocious lead work, followed by the classic "China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider" segue into a rollicking "Casey Jones". The second set keeps the energy high, turning heads with the novelty Beach Boys covers—"Good Vibrations", "I Get Around"—and the fan‑favorite “Okie From Muskogee*”, where Jerry’s guitar shreddes while Pigpen’s organ swells on “Searchin'*”. Highlights close out the night with a soulful "Turn On Your Lovelight", cementing this show as both quirky and quintessentially Dead. The performance is captured best on an excellent soundboard tape that sounds crystal‑clear, letting every nuance of the band’s high‑octane playing shine through.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Five of eight recordings are marked positive, praising the high‑energy set, standout jams (Hard To Handle, Okie From Muskogee*), and strong musicianship; three mixed reviews note only minor flaws (cut-off Help Me Rhonda, uneven Beach Boys medley). Consistent enthusiasm yields a solid +1.4 sentiment boost.

best_recording.reason: Excellent soundboard capture with crystal‑clear, lossless audio and no dropouts; preserves the full performance at studio‑level fidelity.

key_highlights: Legendary Hard To Handle jam that drives the first set forward
Jerry’s incendiary guitar on Okie From Muskogee*
Pigpen’s gritty organ on Searchin'* and soulful Turn On Your Lovelight finale
Unique Beach Boys guest segment adds quirky novelty without killing momentum

band_performance.Jerry: Ripping guitar on Okie From Muskogee* and fiery lead throughout Hard To Handle; consistently praised for fierce fills and soloing.
band_performance.Phil: Provides a solid, anchoring bass foundation that underpins the high‑energy jams, especially during tight transitions.
band_performance.Bob: Delivers powerful vocals on Casey Jones and adds rhythmic depth across the set, keeping the groove locked in.
band_performance.Keys: Pigpen's organ work shines on Searchin'* and adds essential grit to the show; reviewers note his contributions as vital.
band_performance.Drums: The drum work drives the set with steady power, only occasional slip‑ups when tempo eases for guest spots.
